# Acme Logistics

## Journal

Acme Logistics was a separate procurement path from Acme Robotics, driven by warehouse exception handling and cross-dock coordination pain in Benelux. The early deal was modest on purpose and sold as an operations experiment, which means the buying group still thinks in terms of proof and practical value rather than strategic platform language.

Because of the name overlap, this account is easy to conflate with the robotics one if some

# Acme Robotics

## Journal

Acme Robotics came in through a systems integrator intro and responded well to a practical workflow audit instead of a broad product pitch. The underlying pain is the handoff between QA and plant operations, and they tend to engage when the conversation stays anchored in lost time, defect follow-up, and operational drift.
